frozen_components: LLM backbone (4-bit quantized, frozen during QLoRA)
architecture.summary: Sequence-to-sequence encoder-decoder models (mT5, mBART) fine-tuned for cross-lingual aspect-based sentiment analysis. During decoding, a scheme-guided constrained decoding module restricts the token generation space dynamically based on task state (e.g., aspect terms constrained to input sentence tokens, categories to predefined set, polarities to {great, ok, bad}), ensuring output adheres to expected format. Multi-task variants use task-specific markers in the input prompt to handle multiple ABSA tasks with a single model. LLMs (LLaMA 2/3, Orca 2, ChatGPT) are evaluated in zero-shot, few-shot, and QLoRA fine-tuning modes.

biblio.title: Improving Generative Cross-lingual Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis with Constrained Decoding

claims.dataset: SemEval-2016 Task 5 (7 languages: en, es, fr, nl, ru, tr, cs)
claims.statement: Constrained decoding improves cross-lingual performance by 5% on average for the most complex task (TASD)

claims.dataset: SemEval-2016 Task 5 (6 ABSA tasks across 7 languages)
claims.statement: Multi-tasking with constrained decoding boosts results by more than 10%
mT5 with constrained decoding significantly outperforms prior ATE baselines with improvements exceeding 20% and even 30% in most cases

claims.dataset: SemEval-2016 Task 5 (monolingual and cross-lingual E2E-ABSA, ACSA, ACTE, TASD)
claims.statement: LLMs perform poorly in zero-shot and few-shot settings for compound ABSA tasks, but fine-tuning achieves competitive results compared to smaller multilingual models, albeit at the cost of longer training and inference times

Constrained decoding mitigates the problem where models correctly identify aspect terms but in the source language instead of the target language, by restricting available tokens to only those from the input target language sentence
The proposed sequence-to-sequence approach eliminates the need for external translation tools, which are unreliable and may introduce errors in cross-lingual ABSA tasks
For the ACD task, constrained decoding does not significantly improve results since aspect categories are always predicted in English, resulting in no generation problems
Constrained decoding does not significantly enhance monolingual results, as the model has no problems generating the aspect terms in a given source language
